ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly reads 'Gotenks'. Figure wears canonical Metamoran fusion vest (black-and-white cropped vest with white sash), which is exclusive to fusion-dance characters in Dragon Ball lore. Two-tone hair and child-scale frame match Gotenks' standard design. Special Trait and Battle of Gods Saga era designation confirm. Verified via card name, Metamoran vest visual identifier, and Bandai official card database.

trivia_bullets.text: Galactic Battle (BT1), released July 28, 2017, was the first English DBS Card Game booster. Including Gotenks as a common in the inaugural set signals how central fusion characters are to the game's identity — the fusion mechanic would later anchor multiple competitive archetypes across BT2 (Union Force) and beyond. [PSA CardFacts: 2017 Dragon Ball Super Series 1 Galactic Battle]
sources: PSA CardFacts: 2017 Dragon Ball Super Series 1 Galactic Battle

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-071 is a Green Battle card with Power 15,000 and energy cost 3 Green. Its Auto skill reads: 'When this card attacks a Leader Card, your opponent chooses 1 card from their hand and places it in the Drop Area.' At 3 energy, a 15,000-power body is above the curve for cost, and the hand-disruption trigger on Leader attacks means opponents must decide whether to block the attack (preventing the discard) or take the Leader hit and lose a card. [WebSearch: The Side Deck listing BT1-071; TCGplayer product page BT1-071]
sources: The Side Deck: Energy Power Gotenks BT1-071
TCGplayer: Energy Power Gotenks Galactic Battle

trivia_bullets.text: Gotenks is the result of the Fusion Dance performed by Goten and Trunks — a technique Goku learned from the Metamorans in the Other World and taught to his son. All Fusion Dance products wear the canonical Metamoran attire: a black-and-white cropped vest with a white sash, baggy white pants, and black wristbands. The 'cocky confidence' read in the art mirrors Gotenks' established personality — the fusion inherits Trunks' brashness and Goten's fearlessness, producing a child warrior who consistently underestimates his opponents. [Saiyan Warrior: Gotenks character profile; GameRant: Dragon Ball — All Forms of Gotenks Explained]
sources: GameRant: Dragon Ball All Forms of Gotenks Explained
